item.title: High fives all round as Chevron boosts Mediterranean portfolio
item.description: Chevron adds a fifth exploration block to its Greece portfolio

item.title: Industry bullish on meeting red-hot FPSO demand
item.description: Four industry experts discuss demand, complexities, gas FPSOs and whether to redeploy

item.title: Escalating strike action at Ichthys LNG could see production shut in
item.description: Unionised workers extend work to rule, with LNG and condensate loadings impacted

item.title: Adnoc evaluating commercial bids for gas project prize
item.description: At least three contractors still in the race for Waset job

item.title: Full steam ahead at Malaysian offshore CCS project
item.description: Geophysical site survey imminent at Duyong field

item.title: Slow return to normal expected after US-Iran deal, analysts say
item.description: US and Iran to sign framework agreement on Friday to end Middle East war and reopen Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Petrobras seeks ion battery technologies for subsea applications
item.description: Potential use of lithium-ion and sodium-ion batteries to be analysed in Brazil

item.title: Vaalco reports 'impressive' production from Gabon well
item.description: Ebouri field probe flowed 8000 bpd during initial tests

item.title: TotalEnergies: Talks to return massive German offshore wind site have failed so far
item.description: French oil and gas major in 2024 pledged to pay German state $2.3 billion for right to build and operate North Sea offshore wind farm

item.title: UK carbon storage expert recognised in King's Birthday Honours
item.description: UK has permitted four carbon stores as part of the government's strategy to reduce greenhouse gas emissions

item.title: Player's stock price hits all time low after Syria deal
item.description: Shareholders in AXP Energy downbeat over onshore farm-in agreement

item.title: How the EU's growing cyber-anxiety could change wind and solar procurement
item.description: As cybersecurity concerns are driven centre stage, future selection of technology for EU-based renewable energy projects will need to take account of more than just cost or technical suitability, said a security expert

item.title: Commercial e-fuels plant starts operations in the US
item.description: AirPlant One can produce e-naptha and e-SAF, with an offtake agreement for the latter in place with Alaska Airlines and Microsoft

item.title: UK taps Japan for $12 billion floating wind project warchest
item.description: UK government says Japanese investments under Offshore Wind Compact will include developments off Scotland and Wales

item.title: BW Offshore one step closer to Barossa FPSO payday
item.description: Interim Performance Test successfully completed on BW Opal

item.title: New oil volumes confirmed for Johan Sverdrup expansion
item.description: Partners working on phase four at Norway's largest producing oilfield

item.title: Oil prices fall as US and Iran agree peace deal
item.description: Agreement to be signed on Friday will see Strait of Hormuz reopened

item.title: Progress at last on Equinor's landmark Ringvei Vest project
item.description: Combined resources are 350 million barrels of oil equivalent recoverable

item.title: ExxonMobil on the acquisition trail, Woodside Energy in its sights
item.description: Australian player ‘not aware of any proposal’

item.title: Sailaway: Jacket for Neptun Deep departs Saipem’s yard
item.description: Neptun Deep is largest offshore natural gas project under development in the EU

item.title: Australian duo moving ahead with Mongolian gas projects
item.description: Australian operators looking to progress their onshore coal bed methane gas projects

item.title: Medco Energi secures FPSO for less than $9 million
item.description: Payment guarantee intended to ensure smooth transfer of operations at maiden Malaysian asset

item.title: Beetaloo basin shale gas pilot project on the home straight
item.description: Beetaloo Energy's Carpentaria development in Australia almost across the finishing line

item.title: Sable Offshore lays out plan B in event of peace with Iran
item.description: Reopening of Hormuz would mean end to federal emergency production order, but Sable has plan to keep pumping crude

item.title: Kazakhstan authorises second gas processing plant at largest offshore oilfield
item.description: Astana authorises state run operator Qazaqgaz to proceed with the project without strategic foreign partner

item.title: Is UN the best option to resolve Cambodia-Thailand maritime dispute?
item.description: UN conciliation to resolve the OCA dispute between Cambodia and Thailand would not be legally binding

item.title: Result of Japan offshore gas exploration well revealed
item.description: Japex was hoping for a significant gas discovery

item.title: Woodside pre-empts Inpex’s Browse deal

item.title: This week's leading oil and gas appointments
BP, Equinor top this week's leading oil and gas appointments
